sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal, according to the Hebrews, to be fitted, joined; more correctly, to lean upon, to lie down; compare the Arab. تكاء Conj. VIII.

Paul. Deu 33:3, וְהֵם תֻּכּוּ לְרַגְלֶךָ “and they (the Israelites) lay down at thy feet,” i.e. at the foot of mount Sinai. Some prefer reading תָּכוּ remain (from the root תּוּךְ Syr. to remain).

I. תְּכוּנָה fem. (from the root כּוּן) a place, Job 23:3.

II. תְּכוּנָה f. (from the root תָּכַן)-

(1) arrangement, disposition, structure, Eze 43:11, i.q. תָּכְנִית Eze 43:10.

(2) splendid equipment, Nah 2:10. Compare תָּכְנִית No. 2.
